enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual weekend update from Boulder, mentioning he was too high to film earlier, that he's been working on his website and YouTube page, is obsessed with motorcycle riding in the mountains, and is on a daily audiobook streak working through titles on history and literature.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: completely obsessed and loves exploring mountain roads and trails
committed to powering through one per day to build familiarity rather than re-listening to the same ones
excited that it's grown bigger than expected, targeting April completion
expects to skip videos again next winter like he did this past one

entities: Confessions of the Child of the Century

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a four day weekend and I was just so high, I was too high to do a video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm embarrassed that I didn't make a video over the weekend.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Over the weekend I worked pretty hard, similar to how I did over the winter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't think I'll be making any videos next coming winter either.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on my website and it's taking on a life of its own.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website is becoming much bigger than I ever imagined it to be.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to write out my website in a minute or the week or so.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm trying to tie everything together.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel totally hooked up on my motorcycle and obsessed with exploring on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went up the very first day that I could legally ride my motorcycle on Friday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to turn around when it started raining.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love that I can get to trails so fast on my motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The gas for my motorcycle doesn't cost that much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited to learn all these roads on my motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm listening to audiobooks every day, aiming to get through a lot of them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently listening to 'Confessions of the Child of the Century' about a dude who lived from 1815-57.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Before that, I listened to 'Report in the Hiroshima all-makes', which was really important and interesting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Before that, I listened to 'Captain Cook' and 'Mary Wilson Crossup'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to finish the current audiobook today and then start another one.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's exciting to listen to audiobooks and power through them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was doing the same thing with the Bible for a while.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to cut out my life home videos, some are easy to get out and some are hard.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's one video on my back that I don't know if I want to go alone.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-LOG. I guess this will be for the weekend of the 22nd and the 23rd because I didn't do video. I had a four-day weekend and I was just so high. I was too high to do video. So now I'm sober. So I guess I'll leave my video.

Over the weekend, I worked pretty hard. It was kind of, I felt like, you know, how I did over the winter. And I don't think I'll be making any videos this next coming winter either. It'll probably be the same thing, just working on my website. Yeah, it's getting, it's cool. It's taking on a life of its own, designing itself.

So, this is what I'm working on. It ended up being a lot bigger than I ever imagined it to be. So, but whatever. Just let it, ride it out. Maybe another week or so. And then I work on my YouTube page. And then some other ones slowly tie together.
So, before I was thinking maybe I'd be done with my personal website by the end of the year. But now I'm thinking by the end of, uh, um, April. End of next April. When I start working again.
Um, so today I've got, uh, I guess one big place to do for me and, uh, come back. I, I, I feel totally hooked on my motorcycle. Like, I'm obsessed with the, with, um, exploring on it.
On all the different roads and trails and stuff. I'm like, oh, I gotta go up now. I gotta go up now. I went up the very first day that I could legally on Friday.
And I had to turn around when it started raining. Um, but if today, dude, whenever there's like a sunny day, I'm up, I think. I'm up there. I love it.
You can get to these trails so fast. You're there. Boom. And the money's not that much. The gas and you can just go everywhere and learn all these roads. I'm so excited.
Uh, so maybe I'll do that today. Um, and, uh, oh, I'm listening to audio books every day. It's like now every day, a new audio book.
So the, the only goal is to just get through an audio book a day, get through a lot, a lot of them. That way I'm at least familiar with the audio books that I have.
So I'm doing one now called Confessions of the Child of the Century. And this dude who lived from 1810 to 1849.
I mean, 1810 to 1857 in France. And he's just a lovesick dude. And it's just his autobiography.
And my job, uh, Johan Maseo or whatever. Charles Maseo or, uh, I can't remember. Anyways, uh, uh, yeah.
And before that I did, uh, the report on the Hiroshima bombings, which is really important and interesting.
And then before that, um, Captain Cook and before that, um, Mary Wilson Craft.
So I'll finish this guy today and then do another one tomorrow.
Woo. It's exciting to do one a day. It's just listen to him and power through.
Instead of the same one over and over again, which, like, I was doing with the Bible for a while.
But, uh, yeah. Maybe I'll do some, uh, YouTube videos.
I want to cut out my lipomas. I've got a couple more lipomas that are easy to get out.
And then I've got a couple more that are hard. I don't know if I want to do it alone.
And then there's one on my neck. I don't know if I want to do that one alone.
But we'll see.
Anyways, okay, that's enough. Peace out.
Peace out.
